title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: It was pleasant I got to eat breakfast and have coffee Wow I thought you were going to say You like spending time with your daughter That too I hope you enjoyed this episode of Lunch Hour English Check out the pinned comment below I will see you all next time Bye

title: will / shall
examples: Oh yeah, way more. I was managing property. I was leasing. I was doing maintenance supervisor work Normally you have one person for each one of those jobs And I did it for three four years It took me from this happy positive person to oh my gosh, I'm stressed I got to do this. I got to do that. There's no time. You feel overwhelmed every day And then I just told myself I need one job and I wasn't able to find it there at that company So that's what prompted me to start checking out the job market It worked out good. It turned out a former boss of mine from years ago called me and offered me this job Nice. Oh, I went with it. Here we are again And I told my boss I only need this job for about four to five years And then hopefully I can retire and then life will slow down a bit I hope maybe not. I'm not interested in climbing corporate ladders anymore And I made that clear right up front When you were job hunting do you have any specific memories?

She took it serious. She's like then so my gosh It'd be great to work with you again. Left it off. I kept thinking about it I worked with her before we worked great Let me interview and see what they have to offer So I went and interviewed so that was kind of a positive one Now all of a sudden this joke turned into a real thing That's good that you put yourself out there It's not what you know it's who you know Word of mouth sometimes is the best It worked out great net instance so far so good so Good. Can I ask you a little bit about the interview process And he's certain questions they asked you how you answered it Anything like that It's a it's a big company that I work for So they have policies that they follow But it all starts with me going online Applying and then they acknowledge that they have my resume My credentials everything like that You get an email saying hey welcome aboard blah blah blah we want you to interview So they set up an interview and there were three people in the room And that was the first interview so we got through that one I assumed good and then I got another call From their human resource director at corporate They wanted to set up a time for an interview Obviously we had to do it virtually And I said hey I have a Friday off anytime during that day So they said okay how about 12 noon I said great I'll be there So 12 noon I got my dress shirt on and everything And nobody was there Did I get my dates wrong?

Our second guests and myself they called me and said We're very sorry we're having technical issues Can we reschedule for later today I said yeah that if you just want to shoot me a text And we'll do the interview I got tired of waiting so I went out and started cutting a tree down All of a sudden they called And I'm all sweaty at this point I've got t-shirt full of sawdust and all this stuff And so I didn't want to miss it though this is my one shot So I ran in the house, took my nasty sweaty sawdust filled t-shirt off Put on a nice dry shirt, go my hair set down Okay there I interviewed with two people from corporate They were asking me all kinds of questions about Why are you leaving your current job?

They couldn't believe it You're the property manager Yes And you do the leasing Yes And you're the maintenance supervisor Yes Job titles So I want to get into a place where I can dedicate my time and energy into one job Right Makes sense So yeah Interview process went well And then following Monday I got a job offer I was happy and nervous Like oh boy All the emotions Yeah because all that happened so fast Now I have to put a two week notice in Or do I even want to jump jobs You start second guessing Yeah you start thinking about it all And then I just kept telling myself Stay the course It's time for a change Don't let your fears get in your way When I put my two week notice in And they didn't accept it right away They were looking to counteroffer What not And I made it known to them That it's time for me to move on They accepted my two week notice And I think it's really brave I know it's scary for anybody to change careers To me it's survival mode Can't stay here You gotta go there All right survival mode kicks in And you just go into overdrive And don't stop until you get a job You have any advice for somebody That's looking to change jobs Especially at a later time in their life Don't think you can't You have more to contribute than what you You will give yourself credit for That's good Yeah just don't be afraid A large part of your career has been in sales Because I remember growing up You were a car salesman Now you're doing leasing for apartments Can you give some advice on How to sell yourself Convince people that you're right for the job I guess honesty is the biggest thing Be genuine Be honest Because people will see that And they gravitate to it So honesty is the best policy Honesty Yeah just be genuine Just be transparent Be upfront in that You never have to lie That's good advice Work for media guys So you mentioned that you want to retire In four or five years hopefully Though I'm just wondering Do you have any plans after retirement Or is something that you want to do After you retire I'll have to stay busy somehow Because I've been busy my entire life I've always wanted to get into some type of broadcasting When I was in high school A senior in high school They had media production and broadcasting And I took that class And it sparked an interest in me Life happened So now maybe when I have a little more time I could maybe dabble in that area I guess news I love current events Maybe a podcast someday Maybe a podcast or definitely a podcast I'll say maybe for now Knowing yourself they definitely Because you're doing it for us Definitely We'd love for you to do A few episodes of your own podcast on our channel Are you sure you want me to?

It was pleasant I got to eat breakfast and have coffee Wow I thought you were going to say You like spending time with your daughter That too I hope you enjoyed this episode of Lunch Hour English Check out the pinned comment below I will see you all next time Bye

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Why are you leaving your current job? What's your goal? I got a job offer. I said Hello everybody, and welcome back to lunch hour English, but today it's more like brunch our English because My dad is an early riser, and I feel like breakfast is his specialty So today we're having sausage and eggs and hash browns all prepared by my dad. Thank you My pleasure. I love these breakfasts. Can we dig in? Get a while. It's hot So dad, can you introduce yourself to the viewers? My name is Vince Anderson. What do you do for work these days dad?
It can be a challenge getting your foot in the door even to get a interview Do you feel like you faced like ageism discrimination because of your age at all during the process?

Maybe inadvertently, but a lot of your corporations now are so versed they've been sued so many times They've learned a lot of lessons on how to keep themselves out of that trouble prepare yourself for rejection You're gonna get rejected here and there you can't stop stopping is not an option because I'm not Retired. I need to still make money. I still need to pay bills So you just keep grinding on until you find that perfect fit What made you leap jobs from your previous job to the job now?

Oh yeah, way more. I was managing property. I was leasing. I was doing maintenance supervisor work Normally you have one person for each one of those jobs And I did it for three four years It took me from this happy positive person to oh my gosh, I'm stressed I got to do this. I got to do that. There's no time. You feel overwhelmed every day And then I just told myself I need one job and I wasn't able to find it there at that company So that's what prompted me to start checking out the job market It worked out good. It turned out a former boss of mine from years ago called me and offered me this job Nice. Oh, I went with it. Here we are again And I told my boss I only need this job for about four to five years And then hopefully I can retire and then life will slow down a bit I hope maybe not. I'm not interested in climbing corporate ladders anymore And I made that clear right up front When you were job hunting do you have any specific memories?

Something that really stuck out to you. It can be something good or something bad Maybe a negative one that I didn't feel good about is When I was moving from Tawas to Saginaw like 11, 12 years ago I was diligently seeking a full-time job in Saginaw because the community's too long And I interviewed with a beverage distributor It was kind of a sales position. You go out to all the restaurants and bars and sell them Beverage. Really intrigued me. I liked it They liked my resume and they called me in for an interview The manager who interviewed me. He was kind of had pity on me that I'm an older guy Just his body language? Yeah, his body language his eyebrows like Oh, hi Mr. Andrew. Please sit up You could tell it was just a superficial, artificial And we got done with the interview and he cut it kind of short and he stood up and We were walking to the door and he patted me on the back Okay Mr. Anderson. Thanks for coming in and I stopped and looked at him I said why don't you just tell me I didn't get the job. I didn't get the job And that's okay, but that pad on the back was almost patronizing her Oh look at the old boy. He's out in the job market trying to do his thing You know, I mean, that's how I took it and that could be way wrong But just his whole demeanor during the interview and everything So that was kind of a negative thing. Yeah, it felt kind of belittling Yeah I would rather they said hey, we're looking for someone younger But I understand they can't do that because now you might have a lawsuit on your hands The good one was just this past one with my new boss She was looking for a leasing agent and I was looking for a leasing agent I called her and hey, do you run into any good candidates for leasing?
